research: ADSampling (Gao & Long, SIGMOD 2023, arXiv:2303.09855). Random-rotate vectors (JL) then compute squared L2 incrementally in batches of delta; prune a candidate once partial dist res >= threshold * ratio(D,i), ratio = (i/D)*(1+eps0/sqrt(i))^2. Survivors get exact distance (rotation preserves L2). Applied to the EXACT within-cell f32 scan (project core scope). delta=32, eps0=2.1 defaults.
note: ADSampling speeds the exact scan 2.16x at recall 0.999 (eps0=2.1), 1.43x fully LOSSLESS (eps0=3.0, recall 1.0), up to 2.87x at 0.985 (eps0=1.5). Speedup below theoretical (prune after ~32-64 of 1024 dims) due to memory-access granularity (skipping a row's tail saves partial bandwidth) + per-batch branch + heap overhead. Doesn't beat the binary funnel on QPS (20 vs 851) but reaches HIGHER recall (exact/0.999) than binary can - it's the high-recall end of the Pareto. eps0 is the recall/speed dial.
